title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Contract Workshop
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Untrusted Input Defense
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 1736 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Production Defense
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-5rmsas
# Competency: bookbot-production-defense.
# Case 9194: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_production_defense_5rmsas(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_5rmsas = "5rmsas-sasmr5-9194"
    assert evidence_variant_5rmsas
    evidence_axes_5rmsas = (
        "corpus5rmsas",
        "policysasmr5",
        "revision9194",
        "command5rmsas9194",
        "reportsasmr59194",
        "test91945rmsas",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_5rmsas)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
